title: Être, avoir, aller, faire — Key irregular verbs
objective: Use the four essential irregular verbs in the present (A1: can use high-frequency verbs to handle basic needs).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: French says “I HAVE hunger”, not “I am hungry”
bridge.body: For states like hunger, thirst and age, French uses **avoir**, not **être**: **j'ai faim** (lit. “I have hunger”), **j'ai 20 ans** (lit. “I have 20 years”). English uses “to be”, which trips up learners — ✗ *je suis faim*, ✗ *je suis 20 ans*. Hindi actually helps here: मुझे भूख है (“to me there is hunger”) and मेरी उम्र 20 साल है both avoid “I am”, just like French. Memorise the four verbs — they appear everywhere.

quiz.prompt: What is the main bridge idea for "Être, avoir, aller, faire — Key irregular verbs"?
options: Focus on: French says “I HAVE hunger”, not “I am hungry”

example.englishGloss: I am hungry (lit. I have hunger).

glosses.en: I am hungry (lit. I have hunger).

example.englishGloss: I am going to the market.

glosses.en: I am going to the market.

exercises.prompt: How do you say “I am hungry” in French?
exercises.explanation: French uses avoir for hunger: « j'ai faim » (I have hunger). « je suis faim » is a classic mistake.

exercises.hint: Age uses avoir (il/elle form), not être.
exercises.coachNote: Use the four essential irregular verbs in the present (A1: can use high-frequency verbs to handle basic needs).
exercises.text: Priya is 20 years old: « Elle {{}} vingt ans. »

rubricRules.hint: Age uses avoir in French: say “j’ai vingt ans”, not “je suis … ans”.

hintByLang.hi: फ़्रेंच में उम्र के लिए avoir इस्तेमाल होता है: “j’ai vingt ans”, “je suis … ans” नहीं।
